generated_comment: Arre bhai, company policy bhi fair hona chahiye na. If women get separate period leave and then long maternity leave also, project planning pura disturb hota hai, appraisal cycle pe bhi impact aata hai. Men ke liye koi equivalent benefit nahi, toh thoda imbalance lagta hai, bas wahi bol raha hoon.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as workplace liabilities due to menstruation and maternity, while presenting the bias as a fairness concern. It encodes reverse-discrimination framing and productivity/cost arguments against menstrual and maternity leave.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 44. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 22 year old daughter Sana has been offered a job in Mumbai that requires her to relocate. She is excited about it and has already told her employer she will join. She did not ask you before accepting. You are sitting with your wife after dinner and telling her what you think about Sana accepting a job offer without your permission and what you are going to tell Sana tomorrow and why a girl should not be making these kinds of decisions alone.
